model_implementation_plan.overview: Three reusable intelligence engines power all portals. Each is lightweight, explainable, and built with JavaScript libraries.

models.name: Urgency Classification
models.purpose: Assign urgency level (Low/Moderate/High/Critical) to a patient based on symptoms, age, severity, and risk factors.
models.approach: Rule-based scoring with optional tiny logistic regression (trained on synthetic data).

inputs.name: condition
inputs.description: Mapped to base score: cardiac=95, stroke=98, fracture=60, fever=30, cough=20, default=50

models.logic: score = baseScore[condition] + (age > 60 ? 10 : 0) + (severity > 3 ? 15 : 0); if (score >= 80) level = 'critical'; else if (score >= 50) level = 'moderate'; else level = 'low';
integration: User Portal: Guided Care decision (maps urgency to home/clinic/hospital)
Hospital Portal: Smart Triage (displays urgency in patient list, prioritizes)
Admin Portal: Policy simulation (what-if urgency threshold changes)

models.name: Load Prediction
models.purpose: Predict ER wait time and ICU occupancy for the next hour, used for hospital recommendations and queue management.
models.approach: Multivariate linear regression trained on historical load data (loadHistory collection).
models.training: Uses 200+ synthetic records from loadHistory. Model trained once on app startup using ml-regression-multivariate-linear.
integration: User Portal: Load-aware hospital selector (sorts by predicted wait)
User Portal: Smart Time Recommendation (best time to visit)
Hospital Portal: Predictive Queue Management (displays future load)
Admin Portal: Network Operations Dashboard (aggregated load forecasts)

models.name: Surge Detection
models.purpose: Detect when a hospital or network is entering surge conditions and set status (STABLE/WATCH/CRITICAL).
models.approach: Rule-based thresholds combined with moving averages.
models.logic: if (icuOccupancy > 85 || erWait > 45) return 'CRITICAL'; else if (admissionRate > movingAvgAdmission * 1.5) return 'WATCH'; else return 'STABLE';
integration: Hospital Portal: Surge Management page (local status + recommendations)
Admin Portal: Surge Command Center (network-wide status map)

principles: Reusability: One model powers multiple features across all three portals.
Explainability: Rules and linear regression are transparent; no black boxes.
Lightweight: Models run in the browser; no server-side ML needed.
Extensibility: Can be swapped with more advanced models later without changing architecture.
